figures.caption: Table 1: Results on the MMEB-V2 benchmark. The results in bold represent the best performance among different model sizes. CLS: classification, QA: question answer, RET: retrieval, GD: grounding, MRET: moment retrieval, VDR: ViDoRe, VR: VisRAG, OOD: out-of-distribution. Reported metrics adhere to the settings of VLM2Vec-V2. Detailed results can be found in Appendix.
Table 7: Statistics of Initial Sampling and CoT-guided Filtering.
Figure 4: Text-to-Rewrite (T2R) and Image-to-Rewrite (I2R) Prompt Example.
Figure 9: Examples of Data Construction.
Table 4: Comparison between the rewrite and CoT reasoning on MMEB-V2 benchmark. All CoT variants are trained and evaluated based on UME-R1 for fair comparison.

Table 2: Results on the MRMR benchmark. We report nDCG@10 for all subtasks except Negation, for which we use Hit@1: Art, Medicine (Med.), Science (Sci.), Humanities (Hum.), Math, Physics (Phy.), Engineering (Eng.), Business (Bus.), Negation (Neg.), Design (Des.), and Traffic (Tra.). All denotes the average score across 11 subtasks.
Figure 10: Examples of Data Construction.
Table 11: Detailed results of baselines and RIME on Video and Visual Doc of MMEB-v2 benchmark.
Figure 2: The complete multimodal rewrite template and the prompt demo used for SFT cold-start data construction.
Figure 6: Examples of Data Construction.
Table 10: Detailed results of baselines and RIME on MMEB-v2 benchmark. Video and VisDoc results are shown on the next table.

Table 5: Results on the UVRB benchmark. We mainly report Recall@1 for three Tasks: Textual (Txt.), Composed (Cmp.), and Visual (Vis.); three Domains: Coarse-Grained (Coarse-G.), Fine-Grained (Fine-G.), and Long-Context (Long-Ctx.); and three Sub-domains: Spatial (Spa.), Temporal (Temp.), and Partially Relevant (PR.). All is the average across all 16 datasets.

Table 6: Ablation study on embedding modes during inference. Disc. denotes discriminative embeddings, Gen. denotes generative embeddings. Qry. Tokens refers to the average tokens generated per query at retrieval time.

Figure 3: Overview of RIME framework. The framework comprises three core components: (a) Rewrite-Driven Joint SFT that unifies contrastive learning with rewrite generation; (b) Cross-Mode Alignment via inter-mode and intra-mode InfoNCE losses; (c) Refine Reinforcement Learning that guides rewrite policy optimization with multiple rewards.
Figure 7: Examples of Data Construction.
Table 13: Detailed Partition of Datasets in the UVRB Across Tasks, Domains, and Sub-domains
Table 12: Performance of video retrieval on UVRB datasets: AVG values represent the average across 16 datasets.

Table 8: Results on theMR2-Bench benchmark.We report nDCG@10 for all sub-tasks: Biology (Bio.), Cooking (Cook.), Gardening (Gar.), Physics (Phy.), Chemistry (Chem.), Earth Science (Earth.), Economics (Econ.), Mathematics (Math.), Nature (Nat.), Spatial (Spa.), Puzzle (Puzz.), and Analogy (Ana.). All denotes the average score across 12 datasets.
Figure 8: Examples of Data Construction.
Figure 5: Image-Text VQA to Rewrite (IT2R-VQA) and Image-Text Description to Rewrite (IT2R-DESC) Prompt Example.
